note: Wave 6b live Cursor-proxy adjudication of unstructured plain_discovery_*.md (no external judge APIs). Judges applied prompts/judge_referent_clarity.md to prose bodies only.

openai_proxy.rationale: Demonstratives and bare pronouns (they/it/this/that) dominate; only sparse generic anchors remain, forcing repeated inference.

composer_proxy.rationale: Heading states the AML pattern, yet every sentence leans on pronouns without pinning which accounts, corridors, or graph roles are referenced.

openai_proxy.rationale: CTR thresholds and structuring vocabulary ground the AML story; pronouns still blur which depositors versus consolidation accounts execute each hop.

composer_proxy.rationale: Regulatory jargon provides partial anchors, though they/it/them swaps keep counterparties ambiguous from sentence to sentence.

openai_proxy.rationale: Mixer hops, peels, fiat spikes, and wallet clusters mostly name observable entities yet chained they/it tails still muddy upstream custody.

composer_proxy.rationale: Despite pronoun-heavy phrasing, each paragraph restates wallets, hops, fiat ramps, or peel chains so laundering roles largely stay traceable.
